deck: As USAID cancels thousands of contracts and global aid budgets shrink, two Dimagi managing directors share what the funding cuts mean for partners on the ground and how digital tools can stretch every remaining dollar.
seo_description: Dimagi leaders on the aid funding crisis: USAID cuts, the human toll on partners, and how digital tools keep critical health campaigns running with less money.
og_description: Dimagi leaders on the aid funding crisis and how digital tools keep critical health campaigns running with far less money.

pull_quote.text: The aid money can be gone, but the problems are still here. The challenges we're still facing are still here, and we have to find solutions.
pull_quote.cite: Ismaila Diene, Managing Director of Solutions

bullets: The human side of the cuts: partners in Senegal and Madagascar laid off overnight, losing access to email and offices closed down
Stopped campaigns: bed net distribution and vaccination drives delayed or halted as government budgets evaporated
Meeting partners where they are: how Dimagi sticks with governments in the hard times, not just the good ones
Lowering the price of CommCare: a deliberate choice to create value even when it does not help the bottom line
Declaring wartime: Dimagi's internal mode for staying nimble and doing whatever it takes to help partners maximize impact
Pressure creates diamonds: why cost effectiveness, local providers, and proven impact matter more than ever
A word of advice: Ismaila on grounding yourself and focusing on what you can control

stats.label: of USAID's 6,200 multi-year contract awards indicated for elimination
share of global humanitarian assistance funds USAID accounted for in previous years

guests.name: Gillian Javetski
guests.role: Managing Director of Dimagi's Software-as-a-Service division, supporting CommCare Enterprise partners through the funding shifts.

guests.name: Ismaila Diene
guests.role: Managing Director of Dimagi's Solutions division, leading on-the-ground work with government partners running national health campaigns.
